to emit again, typically a previously missed event

Assessment, evaluation, or re-examination
To permit or enable something
Prevent an action from completing when a precondition fails (e.g., refuse with a clear error)
Identify a condition, context, or state and act on it (e.g., detect worktree context, detect malformed input)
Move a piece of software from one platform/language/runtime to another while preserving behavior
extract the essential workflow into reusable commands
Automatically inject content into a target without explicit user invocation
Mark a task as duplicate, obsolete, or otherwise resolved without active implementation work
Release functionality for use, including packaging and any associated rollout steps
Intercept and handle an exception or error condition
Prohibit a behavior or input via tooling enforcement (hook, CLI guard, validator)
Reject an action at runtime, returning an error rather than completing it
Cause a property or invariant to become true (typically via tooling, in titles like 'Make X deterministic')
Replace a manual workflow with code or tooling so it runs without human action.
Remove a file from git's tracking without deleting it from the working tree (git rm --cached)
Assertion, statement, or demand that something is true, factual, or rightfully one's own
Setting free, liberating, or letting go
to associate or attach two things together (e.g., a session to a task)
to break down systematically into components
to take stock of a landscape
to identify similarities and differences
to inspect closely
to form a judgment about
to investigate possibilities in a space
to identify the cause of a problem
Make or become stronger, more secure, or more effective.
Record an entity in a registry so it can be looked up or validated later
Advance to a higher status, rank, level, or visibility
to make stronger, more robust, or more resilient
To strengthen or support something, making it more secure, confident, or effective.
To stimulate someone into action or to coat metal with zinc for protection against corrosion.
Make abstract concrete by treating it as a real thing
To remove something completely from a surface, object, or person.
To reduce in width, scope, range, or focus; to make more limited or specific.
To make certain that something will happen or is the case.
To come back to a topic, decision, or place for renewed examination or reconsideration.
To deliberate over
The act of examining or evaluating something systematically to assess its quality, functionality, or correctness.
To make something longer in length, duration, or scope, or to offer something to another party.
To automatically establish connections or attachments between components or data without manual intervention.
To convert escaped sequences or characters back to their original form.
To move or cause to move from one place, person, or state to another.
To change from one thing to another, or to redirect attention or action to a different option.
To isolate or confine something in a restricted environment to prevent it from affecting the broader system or to test it safely.
To employ or apply something for a specific purpose or action.
To keep or retain something in a place for later use or retrieval.
To recognize, determine, or establish what someone or something is.
To automatically discover or identify something without requiring manual intervention.
To catch, fasten, or attach something using a curved or pointed implement, or to integrate with a system at a designated point of extension.
To present or consider something from a different perspective or context.
To make something smooth and shiny through rubbing, or to improve and refine something to a higher standard.
To come to rest on a surface after descending from the air, or to arrive at and settle in a particular place or state.
Make something conform to or establish a uniform set of rules, specifications, or procedures across multiple instances or contexts.
To examine or study something systematically and carefully to establish facts or discover information.
To harvest or gather crops from a field, or more broadly, to obtain or experience something as a consequence of one's actions.
To cease motion or action, or to bring something to an end.
To control or restrict access to something, or to regulate its availability or release.
To investigate or examine something carefully and thoroughly.
To cut or shape material, typically wood or stone, using a sharp tool.
